sn41.7:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Godatta was staying near Macchikāsaṇḍa in the Wild Mango Grove.
sn41.7:1.2: Then Citta the householder went to him and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side. As he was sitting there, Ven. Godatta said to him,
sn41.7:1.3: “Householder, the immeasurable awareness-release, the nothingness awareness-release, the emptiness awareness-release, the themeless awareness-release: Are these phenomena different in meaning and different in name, or are they one in meaning and different only in name?”
sn41.7:1.4: “Venerable sir, there is a line of reasoning by which these phenomena are different in meaning and different in name,
sn41.7:1.5: and there is a line of reasoning by which they are one in meaning and different only in name.
sn41.7:2.1: “And what is the line of reasoning by which they are different in meaning and different in name?
sn41.7:2.2: There is the case where a monk keeps pervading the first direction [the east] with an awareness imbued with goodwill, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, & all around, everywhere, in its entirety, he keeps pervading the all-encompassing cosmos with an awareness imbued with goodwill—abundant, enlarged, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
sn41.7:2.3: He keeps pervading the first direction with an awareness imbued with compassion…
sn41.7:2.5: equanimity, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, & all around, everywhere, in its entirety, he keeps pervading the all-encompassing cosmos with an awareness imbued with equanimity—abundant, enlarged, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
sn41.7:2.6: This is called the immeasurable awareness-release.
sn41.7:3.2: There is the case where a monk, with the complete transcending of the dimension of the infinitude of consciousness, thinking, ‘There is nothing,’ enters & remains in the dimension of nothingness.
sn41.7:3.3: This is called the nothingness awareness-release.
sn41.7:4.2: There is the case where a monk, having gone into the wilderness, to the root of a tree, or into an empty dwelling, considers this:
sn41.7:4.3: ‘This is empty of self or of anything pertaining to self.’
sn41.7:4.4: This is called the emptiness awareness-release.
sn41.7:5.2: There is the case where a monk, not attending to any theme (object of awareness) enters & remains in the themeless concentration of awareness.
sn41.7:5.3: This is called the themeless awareness-release.
sn41.7:5.4: “This, venerable sir, is the line of reasoning by which these phenomena are different in meaning and different in name.
sn41.7:6.1: “And what, venerable sir, is the line of reasoning by which they are one in meaning and different only in name?
sn41.7:6.2: Passion, venerable sir, is a making of measurement, aversion a making of measurement, delusion a making of measurement.
sn41.7:6.3: For a monk whose effluents are ended these have been abandoned, their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
sn41.7:6.4: To the extent that there are immeasurable awareness-releases, the unprovokable awareness-release is declared supreme.
sn41.7:6.5: And that unprovokable awareness-release is empty of passion, empty of aversion, empty of delusion.
sn41.7:6.6: “Passion is a something, aversion a something, delusion a something.
sn41.7:6.7: For a monk whose effluents are ended these have been abandoned, their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
sn41.7:6.8: To the extent that there are nothingness awareness-releases, the unprovokable awareness-release is declared supreme.
sn41.7:6.9: And that unprovokable awareness-release is empty of passion, empty of aversion, empty of delusion.
sn41.7:6.10: “Passion is a making of themes, aversion a making of themes, delusion a making of themes.
sn41.7:6.11: For a monk whose effluents are ended these have been abandoned, their root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
sn41.7:6.12: To the extent that there are themeless awareness-releases, the unprovokable awareness-release is declared supreme.
sn41.7:6.13: And that unprovokable awareness-release is empty of passion, empty of aversion, empty of delusion.
sn41.7:6.14: “This, venerable sir, is the line of reasoning by which these phenomena are one in meaning and different only in name.”
sn41.7:6.15: “It’s a gain for you, householder, a great gain:
sn41.7:6.16: what your eye of discernment plumbs in the deep word of the Buddha.”
